  • Blog

What Is Juvenile Justice?

Updated April 8, 2024 | Posted December 12, 2020
By the Annie E. Casey Foundation

Juveยญnile jusยญtice in the Unitยญed States is a colยญlecยญtion of state and local court-based sysยญtems whose purยญpose is to respond to young peoยญple who come into conยญtact with law enforceยญment and are accused of breakยญing the law. As part of the legal process, juveยญnile courts hear those casยญes to deterยญmine whether the youth vioยญlatยญed the law and, if so, decide on a propยญer response. State and local juveยญnile corยญrecยญtions agenยญcies (includยญing proยญbaยญtion and resยญiยญdenยญtial cusยญtody) manยญage the rehaยญbilยญiยญtaยญtive proยญgrams, serยญvices and sancยญtions proยญvidยญed to help young peoยญple stop furยญther delinยญquent behavior.

Juveยญnile vs. adult jusยญtice system

Comยญmuยญniยญty safeยญty is a shared goal, but unlike the adult court sysยญtem, the juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem does not recยญogยญnize punยญishยญment as a legitยญiยญmate purยญpose. Rather, its statยญed goal is to help young peoยญple avoid future delinยญquenยญcy and mature into law-abidยญing adults. Toward that end, the juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem has traยญdiยญtionยญalยญly proยญtectยญed the conยญfiยญdenยญtialยญiยญty of court-involved youth.

How many young peoยญple are in the juveยญnile jusยญtice system?

Nationยญal estiยญmates of the numยญber of casยญes processed by juveยญnile courts each year are colยญlectยญed by the Nationยญal Cenยญter for Juveยญnile Jusยญtice. In 2021, the most recent year for which data are availยญable, about 437,000 young peoยญple were referred to juveยญnile courts nationยญwide for delinยญquent offensยญes that vioยญlate the crimยญiยญnal code, and anothยญer 51,500 for staยญtus offensยญes (such as runยญning away, conยญsumยญing alcoยญhol or skipยญping school) that would not be illeยญgal if comยญmitยญted by adults.1

Of the delinยญquenยญcy casยญes, 244,000 (56%) were forยญmalยญly processed in court, of which 118,000 were adjuยญdiยญcatยญed delinยญquent (akin to a guilty conยญvicยญtion in adult court). Among youth who were adjuยญdiยญcatยญed delinยญquent, the largest share (77,000) were placed on proยญbaยญtion, while othยญers (33,000) were removed from home and placed in corยญrecยญtionยญal instiยญtuยญtions or othยญer resยญiยญdenยญtial facilยญiยญties.2

The Cenยญsus Bureau surยญveys resยญiยญdenยญtial facilยญiยญties for juveยญniles to obtain a sinยญgle-day nationยญal count of young peoยญple who are conยญfined in instiยญtuยญtions based on a staยญtus or delinยญquenยญcy offense. Accordยญing to the most recent surยญvey availยญable, on Oct. 27, 2021, there were just under 25,000 youth held in resยญiยญdenยญtial facilยญiยญties, includยญing 11,000 in preยญtriยญal detenยญtion and more than 13,000 comยญmitยญted to resยญiยญdenยญtial facilยญiยญties.3

The 2021 numยญbers of juveยญnile court casยญes and of conยญfined youth are the lowยญest ever recordยญed in these data sources, due in part to the effects of the COVID-19 pandemic. 

What are the steps or stages in the juveยญnile jusยญtice system?

The juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem is a mulยญtiยญstage process: (1) delinยญquent behavยญior, (2) referยญral, (3) intake/โdiversion, (4) transfer/โwaiver, (5) detenยญtion, (6) adjuยญdiยญcaยญtion, (7) disยญpoยญsiยญtion, (8) juveยญnile corยญrecยญtions and (9) aftercare.

  1. Delinยญquent Behavior

    It is develยญopยญmenยญtalยญly norยญmal for teenagers of all races and ethยญnicยญiยญties to be involved in minor acts of delinยญquenยญcy.4 These acts occur in every neighยญborยญhood, but conยญseยญquences vary. In neighยญborยญhoods and schools with very litยญtle police presยญence, delinยญquenยญcy may be unseen by law enforceยญment. Or it may be quickยญly disยญmissed, such as when a police offiยญcer breaks up a parยญty withยญout makยญing any arrests, or when a teacher hanยญdles disยญorยญderยญly conยญduct or in-school fights withยญout involvยญing the jusยญtice sysยญtem. In neighยญborยญhoods and schools with greater police presยญence, youth are more likeยญly to have their delinยญquent behavยญior crimยญiยญnalยญized. Black stuยญdents are more likeยญly than white stuยญdents to attend a school being patrolled by law enforceยญment offiยญcers,5 which conยญtributes to their overยญrepยญreยญsenยญtaยญtion in arrest numbers.

  2. Referยญral

    A young perยญson enters the juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem with an arrest or referยญral. While the vast majorยญiยญty of referยญrals come from police, youth also can be referred by eduยญcaยญtors, parยญents, alleged crime vicยญtims or othยญer memยญbers of the community.

  3. Intake or Diversion

    Once a young perยญson is referred, intake workยญers at the juveยญnile court or proยญbaยญtion agency or attorยญneys in the prosecutorโs office deterยญmine whether the case should be forยญmalยญly processed in juveยญnile court, hanยญdled inforยญmalยญly (divertยญed from court) or dismissed.

  4. Transยญfer or Waiver

    Also at the intake stage, youth accused of very seriยญous offensยญes may be transยญferred (or waived) out of the juveยญnile court to stand triยญal as adults in crimยญiยญnal court. In some states, transยญfers can be ordered by a prosยญeยญcuยญtor, but in most states the transยญfer deciยญsion is made by a juveยญnile court judge in response to a recยญomยญmenยญdaยญtion for transยญfer from the prosยญeยญcuยญtor or intake workยญer. Many states have statuยญtoยญry proยญviยญsions that autoยญmatยญiยญcalยญly transยญfer youth accused of cerยญtain offensยญes, though some of these states also have proยญviยญsions allowยญing for judges to transยญfer youth back to juveยญnile court in at least some cases.

  5. Detenยญtion

    For casยญes forยญmalยญly processed in juveยญnile court, the next deciยญsion is whether to detain the young perยญson until his or her adjuยญdiยญcaยญtion hearยญing, or to perยญmit the young perยญson to remain at home durยญing the pre-adjuยญdiยญcaยญtion periยญod. In most states, judges order preยญtriยญal detenยญtion only when the young perยญson is deemed a danยญger to the comยญmuยญniยญty or a flight risk. Typยญiยญcalยญly, a detenยญtion hearยญing is conยญvened withยญin 24 hours of arrest or othยญer referยญral.

    Juveยญnile casยญes that are forยญmalยญly petiยญtioned in court are more likeยญly to be detained than casยญes that are hanยญdled inforยญmalยญly: in 2021, 36% of forยญmalยญly petiยญtioned casยญes were detained, comยญpared with 13% of casยญes that were inforยญmalยญly hanยญdled.6

  6. Adjuยญdiยญcaยญtion

    In this phase, the young perยญson may be adjuยญdiยญcatยญed delinยญquent, roughยญly equivยญaยญlent to being found guilty in crimยญiยญnal court. Alterยญnaยญtiveยญly, the youth may be divertยญed from the forยญmal sysยญtem to comยญmuยญniยญty-based supยญports, found innoยญcent or the charges may be disยญmissed. As in the adult jusยญtice sysยญtem, the vast majorยญiยญty of casยญes in juveยญnile court are not conยญtestยญed in court. Instead, they are resolved in plea agreeยญments in which the young perยญson admits to a lessยญer charge, or in conยญsent decrees or simยญiยญlar agreeยญments to defer prosยญeยญcuยญtion while the young perยญson adheres to speยญcifยญic conยญdiยญtions. In most casยญes, the conยญdiยญtions include a periยญod of inforยญmal proยญbaยญtion superยญviยญsion. If the case is conยญtestยญed and an adjuยญdiยญcaยญtion hearยญing takes place, a juveยญnile court judge rules based on the eviยญdence preยญsentยญed in court by prosยญeยญcuยญtors and defense attorยญneys. There are no jury triยญals in juveยญnile court.

  7. Disยญpoยญsiยญtion

    After a youth is adjuยญdiยญcatยญed delinยญquent, the next step is a disยญpoยญsiยญtionยญal hearยญing, which is like a senยญtencยญing hearยญing in adult court. Typยญiยญcalยญly, priยญor to this hearยญing, a proยญbaยญtion offiยญcer examยญines the case, interยญviews the young perยญson and develยญops a recยญomยญmendยญed interยญvenยญtion plan. Durยญing the hearยญing, a judge reviews the plan, hears addiยญtionยญal input from prosยญeยญcuยญtion, defense attorยญneys and ideยญalยญly the young perยญson and his/โher famยญiยญly. The judge deterยญmines the disยญpoยญsiยญtion of the case.

  8. Juveยญnile Corยญrecยญtions (Includยญing Proยญbaยญtion and Resยญiยญdenยญtial Custody)

    More than 90% of youth adjuยญdiยญcatยญed delinยญquent are senยญtenced either to comยญmuยญniยญty superยญviยญsion, betยญter known as proยญbaยญtion, or to resยญiยญdenยญtial placement. 

    1. Proยญbaยญtion

      By far the most comยญmon disยญpoยญsiยญtion for youth adjuยญdiยญcatยญed delinยญquent is proยญbaยญtion. Indeed, 65% of all adjuยญdiยญcaยญtions in 2021 โ 77,000 of 118,000 casยญes โ resultยญed in a disยญpoยญsiยญtion to proยญbaยญtion. (In addiยญtion, 41,000 youth who entered into conยญsent decrees or had their casยญes deferred were placed inforยญmalยญly on proยญbaยญtion and 24,000 more youth were placed on inforยญmal proยญbaยญtion after their casยญes were divertยญed from court at intake.) Under proยญbaยญtion arrangeยญments, youth on proยญbaยญtion remain at home under the superยญviยญsion of a proยญbaยญtion offiยญcer and may be required to adhere to rules, parยญticยญiยญpate in mandaยญtoยญry treatยญment activยญiยญties, perยญform comยญmuยญniยญty serยญvice and/โor pay restiยญtuยญtion. Failยญure to comยญply with these rules and requireยญments may result in a proยญbaยญtion vioยญlaยญtion and posยญsiยญble placeยญment into a resยญiยญdenยญtial facilยญiยญty (for those adjuยญdiยญcatยญed and forยญmalยญly disยญposed to proยญbaยญtion) or may result in youth being returned to court, adjuยญdiยญcatยญed delinยญquent and placed either on forยญmal proยญbaยญtion or in a resยญiยญdenยญtial facilยญiยญty (for those who were divertยญed or placed on inforยญmal proยญbaยญtion as part of a conยญsent decree).

    2. Placeยญment

      Just over one-fourth of youth adjuยญdiยญcatยญed delinยญquent in 2021 (28%, about 33,000 young peoยญple) were removed from their homes and placed into resยญiยญdenยญtial facilยญiยญties. These facilยญiยญties vary wideยญly in their charยญacยญterยญisยญtics. Some are large (100 beds or more) and some small (20 beds or fewยญer); some feaยญture corยญrecยญtionยญal designs that closeยญly mirยญror adult prisยญons and some are group homes or resยญiยญdenยญtial treatยญment cenยญters akin to the child welยญfare and menยญtal health sysยญtems; some are locked and/โor fenced, while some are secured only by staff. Some are operยญatยญed by states, while othยญers are operยญatยญed by local govยญernยญments or by priยญvate busiยญnessยญes or nonยญprofยญit organizations.

  9. Afterยญcare

    For youth who are removed from home and placed in a corยญrecยญtionยญal instiยญtuยญtion or othยญer resยญiยญdenยญtial facilยญiยญty after being adjuยญdiยญcatยญed, the final phase of the process may be a periยญod of afterยญcare, where the young perยญson is superยญvised and supยญportยญed durยญing the tranยญsiยญtion back to the community.

Does diverยญsion from the juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem work?

Not all delinยญquenยญcy casยญes referred to the juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem are forยญmalยญly processed in court. Some are disยญmissed, othยญers are divertยญed (hanยญdled inforยญmalยญly outยญside the jusยญtice sysยญtem) and still othยญers are transยญferred for prosยญeยญcuยญtion in adult crimยญiยญnal courts.

Research comยญparยญing the outยญcomes for the youth under difยญferยญent sceยญnarยญios has yieldยญed two priยญmaยญry findings:

  • Diverยญsion โ hanยญdling casยญes outยญside the jusยญtice sysยญtem โ typยญiยญcalยญly leads to betยญter outยญcomes than forยญmal proยญcessยญing in juveยญnile court. The preยญponยญderยญance of eviยญdence indiยญcates that youth whose casยญes are forยญmalยญly processed in juveยญnile court typยญiยญcalยญly have worse outยญcomes than simยญiยญlar youth whose casยญes are hanยญdled inforยญmalยญly, both in terms of future involveยญment in the jusยญtice sysยญtem and sucยญcess in eduยญcaยญtion and employยญment. This is espeยญcialยญly true for youth accused of lowยญer-levยญel misยญbeยญhavยญior and those who do not have a long hisยญtoยญry of past arrests.78
  • Transยญfer or waivยญer to adult court does not reduce rates of subยญseยญquent jusยญtice sysยญtem involveยญment and may increase them. Some studยญies have found that transยญfer has a neuยญtral effect on subยญseยญquent jusยญtice sysยญtem involveยญment,910 while othยญers show that transยญfer leads to worse outยญcomes.1112

Probยญlems in Americaโs juveยญnile jusยญtice system

Though Americaโs juveยญnile courts were foundยญed on noble ideals, they have sufยญfered from seriยญous flaws and endemยญic abusยญes since their foundยญing at the turn of the preยญviยญous century.

  • Wideยญspread vioยญlence and malยญtreatยญment in juveยญnile facilยญiยญties. Since the first juveยญnile reforยญmaยญtoยญries were creยญatยญed in the 19th cenยญtuยญry, facilยญiยญties dedยญiยญcatยญed to housยญing and rehaยญbilยญiยญtatยญing youth have been prone to someยญtimes horยญrifยญic abusยญes. The Casey Founยญdaยญtion has idenยญtiยญfied sysยญtemic or recurยญring vioยญlence in juveยญnile corยญrecยญtions facilยญiยญties across the nation. This trouยญbling eviยญdence shows that large, conยญvenยญtionยญal juveยญnile corยญrecยญtions facilยญiยญties โ or plainยญly statยญed, youth prisยญons โ are inherยญentยญly prone to abuse.
  • Perยญvaยญsive overยญreยญliance on conยญfineยญment, even for youth accused of minor misยญbeยญhavยญior posยญing minยญiยญmal risk to pubยญlic safeยญty. In 1974, when Conยญgress first enactยญed the Juveยญnile Jusยญtice and Delinยญquenยญcy Preยญvenยญtion Act, more than 640,000 youth were admitยญted to juveยญnile detenยญtion or corยญrecยญtions facilยญiยญties, and the daiยญly popยญuยญlaยญtion of youth in conยญfineยญment was 79,000.13 Back then, an estiยญmatยญed 20% of all boys in juveยญnile facilยญiยญties and 70% of all girls were conยญfined for staยญtus offensยญes, not delinยญquenยญcy.14
  • Glarยญing racial and ethยญnic disยญparยญiยญties. Youth of colยญor, espeยญcialยญly Black youth, are subยญject to harshยญer treatยญment than white youth at most every stage of juveยญnile jusยญtice. For instance, Non-Hisยญpanยญic Black youth were 15% of all youth in the Unitยญed States in 2020 but overยญrepยญreยญsentยญed at every stage of the system: 
    • 35% of youth referred to juveยญnile courts for delinquency;
    • 39% of youth forยญmalยญly petiยญtioned in court;
    • 40% of youth placed in preยญtriยญal detention;
    • 42% of youth comยญmitยญted to resยญiยญdenยญtial placeยญment; and
    • 52% of youth waived to stand triยญal as adults in crimยญiยญnal court.15
    Moreยญover, research conยญsisยญtentยญly has found that offendยญing rates difยญfer only modยญestยญly by race and ethยญnicยญiยญty for most offense catยญeยญgories and that difยญferยญences in behavยญior canยญnot explain the overยญrepยญreยญsenยญtaยญtion of youth of colยญor in the jusยญtice sysยญtem.16
  • Failยญure to proยญtect young peopleโs legal rights. Because the juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem was estabยญlished to serve and supยญport youth rather than punยญish them, the courts hisยญtorยญiยญcalยญly operยญatยญed with few proยญceยญdurยญal safeยญguards to proยญtect young peopleโs rights. This often led to egreยญgious malยญtreatยญment of young peoยญple, includยญing lack of legal repยญreยญsenยญtaยญtion, no preยญsumpยญtion of innoยญcence or right to quesยญtion oneโs accusers and harsh punยญishยญment (includยญing incarยญcerยญaยญtion) for behavยญiors that would nevยญer result in simยญiยญlar sancยญtions if comยญmitยญted by adults. The U.S. Supreme Court addressed many of these flaws in a series of deciยญsions in the 1960s and 1970s.

How has the juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem made progress?

Americaโs juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem has made encourยญagยญing progress in recent times, at least through 2021. These gains include:

  • A nationยญal moveยญment for detenยญtion reform. Launched in 1992, the Annie E. Casey Foundationโs Juveยญnile Detenยญtion Alterยญnaยญtives Iniยญtiaยญtiveยฎ (JDAI) has helped jurisยญdicยญtions throughยญout the counยญtry sharply reduce reliance on pre-triยญal detenยญtion through core strateยญgies, such as greater use of objecยญtive deciยญsion makยญing and effecยญtive alterยญnaยญtives to detenยญtion. JDAIยฎ has reached more than 300 counยญties in 40 states nationยญwide, home to roughยญly one-third of the nationโs adoยญlesยญcents. Many of JDAIโs eight core prinยญciยญples for detenยญtion have become stanยญdard pracยญtices throughยญout the nation. In recent years, JDAIโs focus has expandยญed to othยญer parts of the juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem, includยญing reducยญing the use of conยญfineยญment folยญlowยญing adjuยญdiยญcaยญtion and transยญformยญing juveยญnile proยญbaยญtion practices.
  • Betยญter underยญstandยญing about adoยญlesยญcent behavยญior and brain develยญopยญment. Breakยญthroughs in brain imagยญing techยญnoloยญgies and behavยญioral sciยญence show that the human brain doesnโt fulยญly develยญop until age 25. From adoยญlesยญcent brain develยญopยญment research, we underยญstand why adoยญlesยญcents are more prone than adults to risk-takยญing and law-breakยญing behavยญiors, and why most will age out of these behavยญiors natยญuยญralยญly withยญout any interยญvenยญtion from the court. This research has spurred a series of Supreme Court deciยญsions outยญlawยญing the death penalยญty and life withยญout parole senยญtences for crimes comยญmitยญted durยญing adoยญlesยญcence, as well as new laws in many states to raise the maxยญiยญmum age of the juveยญnile courtโs jurisยญdicยญtion because they see clear benยญeยญfits of keepยญing youth out of the adult crimยญiยญnal jusยญtice system.
  • New eviยญdence on what works. Over the past four decades, scholยญars have amassed eviยญdence about what does and doesnโt work to steer young peoยญple away from delinยญquenยญcy, includยญing both genยญerยญal prinยญciยญples and strateยญgies for how to interยญvene effecยญtiveยญly with youth and speยญcifยญic eviยญdence-based interยญvenยญtion proยญgram modยญels with proven sucยญcess in reducยญing young peopleโs reofยญfendยญing rates.1718 Scholยญars also have docยญuยญmentยญed the powยญerยญful effects of earยญly childยญhood trauยญma in the lives of many youth who become enmeshed in the juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem, and they have develยญoped pracยญtices that help youth address and heal from trauยญmatยญic expeยญriยญences in their lives.19
  • A big drop in juveยญnile incarยญcerยญaยญtion. Since juveยญnile conยญfineยญment peaked in 1999 at more than 107,000 young peoยญple, the numยญber of young peoยญple held in resยญiยญdenยญtial facilยญiยญties as a result of delinยญquent conยญduct has declined steadiยญly. By 2021, the last year for which data are availยญable, total conยญfineยญment on a sinยญgle day was under 25,000 โ down 77% from the 1999 high. While much of the decline was due to subยญstanยญtial reducยญtion in juveยญnile arrests, espeยญcialยญly for seriยญous vioยญlent offensยญes, many states have begun to limยญit the use of conยญfineยญment, espeยญcialยญly for less seriยญous offensยญes.2021 Many statehave been closยญing large juveยญnile corยญrecยญtionยญal instiยญtuยญtions (someยญtimes called trainยญing schools, but more accuยญrateยญly described as youth prisยญons). From 2000 to 2020, the numยญber of juveยญnile facilยญiยญties housยญing more than 100 young peoยญple fell by more than 80% from 264 to 44, and the numยญber of youth housed in these large facilยญiยญties fell 91%.22 Over the same periยญod, the share of young peoยญple housed in overยญcrowdยญed facilยญiยญties (where the numยญber of youth exceeds the numยญber of perยญmaยญnent beds) fell from 20% in 2000 to just 2% in 2020.

What are some chalยญlenges with the juveยญnile jusยญtice system?

While it has made progress, our nationโs juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtem still faces urgent chalยญlenges such as:

  • Racial and ethยญnic disยญparยญiยญties are getยญting worse. Whereยญas Black youth nationยญwide were conยญfined at four times the rate of white youth in 2001, by 2021 the Black rate of conยญfineยญment had grown to 4.7 times the rate for white youth.23
  • The Unitยญed States locks up more young peoยญple than othยญer counยญtries do. Accordยญing to the 2019 Unitยญed Nations Globยญal Study on Chilยญdren Deprived of Libยญerยญty, the U.S. youth conยญfineยญment rate was four times highยญer than Canaยญda and Mexยญiยญco; 10 times highยญer than cenยญtral and eastยญern Europe; and 12 times highยญer than westยญern Europe.24
  • Corยญrecยญtionยญal conยญfineยญment facilยญiยญties remain danยญgerยญous and probยญlemยญatยญic, rather than rehaยญbilยญiยญtaยญtive. Sysยญtemic or recurยญring malยญtreatยญment of conยญfined youth had been docยญuยญmentยญed in the juveยญnile corยญrecยญtions facilยญiยญties of 29 states plus the Disยญtrict of Columยญbia in research from 2000 through 2015.
  • Juveยญnile proยญbaยญtion, the most comยญmon response to delinยญquenยญcy, often does not operยญate accordยญing to best pracยญtice. In 2021, roughยญly 142,000 young peoยญple were placed on some form of juveยญnile proยญbaยญtion. Yet research finds that proยญbaยญtion is inefยญfecยญtive in reversยญing delinยญquent behavยญior and has espeยญcialยญly poor results with youth at low risk of re-arrest. Many comยญmon pracยญtices in proยญbaยญtion are probยญlemยญatยญic or counterproductive.
  • Far too many U.S. youth are arrestยญed and referred to court, and far too few of those youth are divertยญed from court folยญlowยญing arrest or referยญral. Also, there is far too litยญtle investยญment in effecยญtive comยญmuยญniยญty-driยญven diverยญsion interยญvenยญtions to assist youth who are sufยญferยญing with trauยญma, famยญiยญly crises and seriยญous behavยญioral health chalยญlenges and to preยญvent their slide into seriยญous delinquency.

Time Lag for Fedยญerยญal Data is Two Years or More. What are More Curยญrent Indications?

Nationยญal data for 2022 and 2023 are not yet availยญable, but more recent data from Caseyโs Monthยญly Youth Detenยญtion Surยญvey sugยญgest that juveยญnile court involveยญment and youth conยญfineยญment have increased since 2021. In fact, most jurisยญdicยญtions covยญered by that surยญvey had more young peoยญple in detenยญtion at the start of 2024 than they did four years earยญliยญer (before the panยญdemยญic). That increase was caused by the fact that those admitยญted to detenยญtion are stayยญing much longer than they did before the panยญdemยญic โ not by more young peoยญple being admitยญted to detenยญtion. Youth of colยญor, espeยญcialยญly Black youth, have sufยญfered the most from these changes. Before the panยญdemยญic, Black youth in the jurisยญdicยญtions covยญered by the surยญvey were about six times more likeยญly than white youth to be in detenยญtion, simยญiยญlar to the 2019 nationยญal averยญage. But by earยญly 2024, they were about eight times more likeยญly to be detained, a highยญer disยญparยญiยญty ratio than has ever appeared in the nationยญal data going back to 1997.

Addiยญtionยญal resources on juveยญnile justice

  • Juveยญnile detention
  • Jueยญnile incarceration
  • Juveยญnile probation
  • Diverยญsion and prevention

Law & Inequalยญiยญty: A Jourยญnal of Theยญoยญry and Pracยญtice, 31(2). Reno, NV: Nationยญal Counยญcil of Juveยญnile and Famยญiยญly Court Judges. Retrieved from wwwโ.ncjfcjโ.org/โpโuโbโlโiโcโaโtโiโoโnโsโ/โtโeโnโ-โtโhโiโnโgโsโ-โeโvโeโrโyโ-โjโuโvโeโnโiโlโeโ-โcโoโuโrโtโ-โjโuโdโgโeโ-โsโhโoโuโlโdโ-โkโnโoโwโ-โaโbโoโuโtโ-โtโrโaโuโmโaโ-โaโnโdโ-โdโeโlโiโnโqโuency โฉ
20. Durยญnan, J., Olsen, R., Harvell, S. (2018). State-led juveยญnile jusยญtice sysยญtems improveยญment: Impleยญmenยญtaยญtion progress and earยญly outยญcomes. Washยญingยญton, DC: Urban Instiยญtute. Retrieved from wwwโ.urbanโ.org/โrโeโsโeโaโrโcโhโ/โpโuโbโlโiโcโaโtโiโoโnโ/โsโtโaโtโeโ-โlโeโdโ-โjโuโvโeโnโiโlโeโ-โjโuโsโtโiโcโeโ-โsโyโsโtโeโmโsโ-โiโmโpโrโoโvโement โฉ
21. The Nationยญal Juveยญnile Jusยญtice Netยญwork & the Texas Pubยญlic Polยญiยญcy Founยญdaยญtion. (2013). The comeยญback states: Reducยญing juveยญnile incarยญcerยญaยญtion in the Unitยญed States.
